qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing authorization in CartController._getCart() allows unauthenticated access to arbitrary carts
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes blind SQL injection via hasVariant/hasProduct properties bypassing the blocklist in ElementIndexesController (CWE-89). The finding describes IDOR/missing authorization in CartController._getCart() allowing unauthenticated cart access - a completely different vulnerability class, root cause, and code location.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes blind SQL injection via hasVariant/hasProduct properties bypassing the blocklist in ElementIndexesController (GHSA-2453-mppf-46cj bypass). The only qualifying finding describes IDOR/missing authorization in CartController._getCart() - a different vulnerability class (CWE-639 vs CWE-89), different code location (CartController vs ElementIndexesController/ProductQuery/VariantQuery), and different root cause (missing authorization check vs unsanitized query configuration via Craft::configure()).
evidence: Advisory: SQL injection via hasVariant/hasProduct in ProductQuery/VariantQuery bypassing ElementIndexesController blocklist
Finding: IDOR via missing authorization in CartController._getCart() on cart endpoints
Different CWE classes: CWE-89 (SQL injection) vs CWE-639 (IDOR/missing authorization)
